$schema_note: Acknowledged security findings. DeepSeek workflow skips these when counting CRITICAL/HIGH blockers. Each entry has a title pattern (case-insensitive substring match) and explanation.

acknowledged.reason: MITIGATED: MAX_WORKER_THREADS=256 cap + thread::Builder (no panic on spawn fail)
MITIGATED: MAX_DNS_RESOLVER_THREADS=4, 3s timeout, slot cleanup on spawn failure
MITIGATED: same as DNS Resolver — bounded pool with timeout
MITIGATED: global_orphan_byte_limit() + per-block size check + checked_add at insertion
MITIGATED: same as Orphan Block Pool — byte limit + eviction + size check
MITIGATED: checked_add used everywhere, saturating_add only for non-overflow-sensitive counters
MITIGATED: orphan pool operations protected by mutex, no concurrent access to byte counter. Covers: 'race condition in global orphan', 'non-atomic update of global orphan'.
MITIGATED: non-devnet requires explicit --genesis-file + handshake validates genesis_hash (Go parity)
MITIGATED: compact_size parsing enforces MAX_INV_ITEMS before allocation
ACCEPTED_RISK: lock held during message processing bounded by MAX_RELAY_MSG_BYTES + block validation. Splitting requires major async refactor — deferred to post-MVP.
MITIGATED: OUTBOUND_SLOT_RESERVE=2 prevents inbound from starving outbound dials

acknowledged.title_pattern: Transaction ID Malleability Due to Non-Canonical Parsing
acknowledged.reason: FALSE_POSITIVE: matches the exact DeepSeek title. Go canonicalTxID (handlers_tx.go:41) uses identical consumed!=len check. Zero divergence — both reject trailing bytes.

acknowledged.title_pattern: Consensus Divergence Risk in Transaction ID Calculation
acknowledged.reason: FALSE_POSITIVE: matches the exact earlier DeepSeek title for the same parser concern. Rust canonical_txid mirrors Go canonicalTxID exactly (consumed != len → error).
ACCEPTED_RISK: Go parity — Go also has no per-peer send queue limit. Each frame ~70 bytes (INV). Real risk LOW at current throughput. Follow-up: add per-peer cap.
FALSE_POSITIVE: Go handleTx (handlers_tx.go:9-34) also does NOT submit to consensus mempool — only relay pool + re-announce. Mempool admission is a separate RPC path.

acknowledged.title_pattern: Invalid Transactions Stored in Relay Pool
acknowledged.reason: FALSE_POSITIVE: same Go-parity rationale as Transaction Relay Without Validation — the relay pool is intentionally separate from consensus mempool admission on both Rust and Go paths.

acknowledged.title_pattern: Network transactions not submitted to mempool
acknowledged.reason: FALSE_POSITIVE: Go parity — network tx relay does not imply consensus mempool admission. Rust mirrors Go by relaying through the relay pool and keeping mempool admission as a separate path.

FALSE_POSITIVE: Go parity — relay pool storage is intentionally decoupled from consensus mempool validation, so this is the same reviewed false-positive family as Transaction Relay Without Validation.
ACCEPTED_RISK: Go parity — Go MemoryTxPool also count-based (1000) without byte limit. MAX_RELAY_MSG_BYTES limits individual tx size.

acknowledged.title_pattern: Unbounded Relay Pool Growth Under Attack
acknowledged.reason: ACCEPTED_RISK: same reviewed risk family as Memory Exhaustion via Large Transactions — Go parity, bounded entry count, and MAX_RELAY_MSG_BYTES cap keep this as a non-blocking follow-up rather than a merge blocker.
ACCEPTED_RISK: poison = thread panic = node already degraded. Returning error is correct — no recovery possible from panicked thread.
MITIGATED: MAX_GETDATA_RESPONSE_BYTES (128MB) + MAX_GETDATA_RESPONSE_BLOCKS (16) cap. Pre-existing design, not new in this PR.

acknowledged.title_pattern: Mempool Not Updated with P2P Transactions
acknowledged.reason: FALSE_POSITIVE: Go parity — P2P relay plumbing is separate from consensus mempool accounting. The reviewed claim conflates relay propagation with mempool lifecycle management.
FALSE_POSITIVE: same Go-parity rationale as the tx_relay mempool comments — P2P relay intentionally does not imply immediate consensus mempool admission.

acknowledged.title_pattern: Fee=0 for RPC Transactions Causes Priority Distortion
acknowledged.reason: ACCEPTED_RISK: Go relay also uses relay-specific metadata, not consensus fee. fee=0 for RPC-submitted txs matches Go where RelayMetadata is separate from mempool fee.

acknowledged.title_pattern: Fee=0 for RPC Transactions Causes Priority Distortion
acknowledged.reason: ACCEPTED_RISK: same reviewed relay-metadata tradeoff as the tx_relay finding — Go parity keeps relay metadata separate from consensus fee accounting.
ACCEPTED_RISK: O(n) worst-tx selection in relay_pool matches Go implementation and is bounded by the 1000-entry relay pool cap. Not a blocker.
ACCEPTED_RISK: tx_seen eviction strategy matches Go implementation; worst-case scan is bounded by the fixed seen-set capacity and is not a merge blocker.
ACCEPTED_RISK: single parse per tx relay path. Optimizing to reuse parsed tx is premature — relay is not hot path.
